name: TAO Failed Breakdown Reclaim v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing failed breakdown reclaim, but rewritten as a broader value-repair expression rather than the already-killed strict hold grammar. The edge should come from downside rejection plus fast reclaim, not from blind bottom-fishing.
invalidation_condition: Abort if failed-breakdown repairs remain too sparse, if fast reclaim still lacks enough extension after costs, or if the move simply reverts back into weakness.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks below short-term support, quickly reclaims value, and then extends modestly as the failed breakdown is repaired.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In chop, failed breakdowns should occur more often than clean continuation trends. A broader reclaim expression should participate more honestly than the previously killed strict hold sequence.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is a fair structurally different test lane because failed breakdowns and sharp repairs are more visible on a volatile asset.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If failed breakdowns only bounce weakly and do not extend, the reclaim will still lack enough post-cost edge.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries engage but the second leg is too brief, reduce target ambition before altering the reclaim condition.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; breakdown and reclaim are sequential and broad
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reclaim logic instead of exact candle choreography

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by selling 15%
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.15 * ATR_14 by covering 15%
